---
title: "OJS reference — reactive slider"
description: "Minimal Observable JS pattern: viewof input plus a downstream cell that recomputes."
---

This is a reference Observable JS widget. The pattern is: a `viewof` cell exposes a reactive value; downstream cells use that value and recompute automatically when it changes.

```{ojs}
viewof alpha = Inputs.range(
  [0.05, 0.95],
  { value: 0.4, step: 0.05, label: "α" }
)

curve = {
  const xs = d3.range(0.1, 10, 0.1);
  const ys = xs.map(x => Math.pow(x, alpha));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 600,
    height: 300,
    x: { label: "x" },
    y: { label: `x^${alpha.toFixed(2)}` },
    marks: [
      Plot.line(xs.map((x, i) => ({x, y: ys[i]})), {x: "x", y: "y", stroke: "steelblue"})
    ]
  });
}
```

For "drag-a-point, watch-the-construction-update" dynamic geometry, prefer JSXGraph instead — see `../jsxgraph/`.
